ddls:
CREATE TABLE "protein_families" (
  "family_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each protein family */,
  "family_name" TEXT /* Name of the protein family */,
  "family_accession" TEXT /* Accession number for the protein family */,
  "family_description" TEXT /* Description of the protein family */,
  "family_classification" TEXT /* Classification of the protein family */,
  "family_function" TEXT /* Function of the protein family */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protein_subfamilies" (
  "subfamily_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each protein subfamily */,
  "family_id" TEXT /* ID of the protein family the subfamily belongs to */,
  "subfamily_name" TEXT /* Name of the protein subfamily */,
  "subfamily_description" TEXT /* Description of the protein subfamily */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subfamily_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protein_sequences" (
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protein sequence */,
  "sequence_name" TEXT /* Name of the protein sequence */,
  "family_id" TEXT /* ID of the protein family the sequence belongs to */,
  "subfamily_id" TEXT /* ID of the protein subfamily the sequence belongs to */,
  "aligned_sequence" TEXT /* Aligned sequence of the protein */,
  "sequence" TEXT /* Original sequence of the protein */,
  "sequence_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sequence type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_protein_sequences_subfamily_id FOREIGN KEY ("subfamily_id") REFERENCES protein_subfamilies ("subfamily_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_protein_sequences_sequence_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_type_id") REFERENCES sequence_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_protein_sequences_family_id FOREIGN KEY ("family_id") REFERENCES protein_families ("family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequence type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sequence type (e.g., aligned, unaligned) */,
  "type_description" TEXT /* Description of the sequence type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "family_accessions" (
  "accession_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each family accession */,
  "family_id" TEXT /* ID of the protein family the accession belongs to */,
  "accession_number" TEXT /* Accession number for the protein family */,
  "accession_description" TEXT /* Description of the family accession */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("accession_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_family_accessions_family_id FOREIGN KEY ("family_id") REFERENCES protein_families ("family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_names" (
  "name_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequence name */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the protein sequence the name belongs to */,
  "sequence_name" TEXT /* Name of the protein sequence */,
  "name_description" TEXT /* Description of the sequence name */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("name_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_names_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES protein_sequences ("sequence_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the protein sequence being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the sequence */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the sequence was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES protein_sequences ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_similarities" (
  "similarity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each similarity event */,
  "sequence_id_1" INTEGER /* ID of the first protein sequence */,
  "sequence_id_2" INTEGER /* ID of the second protein sequence */,
  "similarity_score" REAL /* Similarity score between the two sequences */,
  "alignment_method" TEXT /* Method used for alignment (e.g., BLAST, ClustalW) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("similarity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_similarities_sequence_id_1 FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id_1") REFERENCES protein_sequences ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_similarities_sequence_id_2 FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id_2") REFERENCES protein_sequences ("sequence_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_annotations" (
  "annotation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each annotation */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the protein sequence the annotation belongs to */,
  "annotation_type" TEXT /* Type of annotation (e.g., functional, structural) */,
  "annotation_text" TEXT /* Text of the annotation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("annotation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_annotations_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES protein_sequences ("sequence_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feature */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the protein sequence the feature belongs to */,
  "feature_type" TEXT /* Type of feature (e.g., motif, domain) */,
  "feature_description" TEXT /* Description of the feature */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_features_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES protein_sequences ("sequence_id")
)
